title: Misleading error message when importing JSON with upper/lowercased keys
description:
### What happens?

When importing json with keys that only differ by case, we can get this message:
```
Not implemented Error: Duplicate name "Name" in struct auto-detected in JSON, try ignore_errors=true
```
This can be misleading and confusing, since the proplem is really that there are two different keys that differ only in their case.

### To Reproduce

```sql
create table test as select * from read_json('test.json', format='auto', ignore_errors=false);
```

where `test.json` has

```json
[
  {
  "keyA" : [
        {"name": "foo"},
        {"Name": "bar"}
        ]
  }
]
```

### OS:

MacOS Sonoma

### DuckDB Version:

1.0.0

### DuckDB Client:

DuckDB command-line

bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE json_test (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, keyA VARCHAR);
CREATE TABLE json_test2 (id INTEGER, name VARCHAR, Name VARCHAR);
INSERT INTO json_test2 SELECT id, JSON_VALUE(keyA, '$[0].name'), JSON_VALUE(keyA, '$[1].Name') FROM json_test;
SELECT * FROM json_test2 WHERE name IS NOT NULL OR Name IS NOT NULL;
key_bug_features: Importing JSON with case-sensitive duplicate keys
